named_docs.name: Risk assessment process for covered systems
named_docs.context: the Secretary shall establish a risk assessment process that holistically evaluates each unique implementation by the Department of a covered system

named_docs.name: Progress reports to Congress
named_docs.context: the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the progress of the Secretary in carrying out this Act

named_docs.name: Annual submissions to Congress
named_docs.context: the Secretary shall submit to Congress an annual submission that includes the ledger...annual report on the findings...annotations made

named_docs.name: public reporting and audit requirements
named_docs.context: public reporting and audit requirements of sections 104 and 105

named_docs.name: State judiciary reporting on facial recognition court orders
named_docs.context: each State judge who has issued a court order authorizing or approving facial recognition in conjunction with a reference photo database shall report to a State agency

named_docs.name: State prosecutor reporting on facial recognition searches
named_docs.context: each State prosecutor shall report to a State agency the following information with respect to the use of facial recognition in conjunction with a reference photo database

named_docs.name: Public report on facial recognition use
named_docs.owner_org: Administrative Office of the United States Courts
named_docs.context: the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts shall release to the public, post online, and transmit to the Congress a full and complete report concerning the use of facial recognition

named_docs.name: Federal audit of facial recognition use
named_docs.context: annually submit data with respect to their use of facial recognition for audit by the Government Accountability Office

named_docs.name: State level audit findings and violations report
named_docs.context: shall report a summary of the findings of the audit, including the number and nature of violations identified, to Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts

named_docs.name: Disaggregated facial recognition data by demographics
named_docs.owner_org: Government Accountability Office; independent State agency
named_docs.context: Data collected pursuant to subsection (a) or (b) shall, when feasible, be collected in a manner that allows such data to be disaggregated by race, ethnicity, gender, and age

named_docs.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology's benchmark facial recognition test for law enforcement
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: annually submitted to the National Institute of Standards and Technology's benchmark facial recognition test

named_docs.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology's training protocol for operational testing
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: in accordance with National Institute of Standards and Technology's training protocol for operational testing

named_docs.name: accuracy or bias reports
named_docs.context: accuracy or bias reports required under this Act

named_docs.name: court order authorizing facial recognition use
named_docs.context: the order that authorized the use of facial recognition

named_docs.name: probe image and modifications documentation
named_docs.context: each probe image that was used by the agency; any modifications made to the probe image

named_docs.name: facial recognition system candidate list
named_docs.context: the candidate list, in rank order, produced by the facial recognition system

named_docs.name: best practices for law enforcement agencies to evaluate the accuracy and fairness of their facial recognition systems
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: develop best practices for law enforcement agencies to evaluate the accuracy and fairness of their facial recognition systems

named_docs.name: benchmark facial recognition test for law enforcement
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: develop and offer an ongoing benchmark facial recognition test for law enforcement that conducts evaluations of actual algorithms used by law enforcement agencies

named_docs.name: operational testing protocol for annual operational testing
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: develop an operational testing protocol that independent testers and law enforcement agencies may implement for annual operational testing to determine the accuracy of the facial recognition system

named_docs.name: training standards for human operators reviewing facial recognition searches
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: study and develop training standards for human operators reviewing the results of facial recognition searches to ensure accuracy and prevent bias

named_docs.name: policy governing the agency's use of facial recognition systems
named_docs.context: establish and make publicly available on the internet website of such agency a policy governing the agency's use of facial recognition systems

named_docs.name: Computational Forensic Algorithm Testing Standards
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: establishment of Computational Forensic Algorithm Testing Standards and a Computational Forensic Algorithm Testing Program

named_docs.name: Computational Forensic Algorithm Testing Program
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: establishment of Computational Forensic Algorithm Testing Standards and a Computational Forensic Algorithm Testing Program

named_docs.name: Protecting the Information of our Vulnerable Adolescents, Children, and Youth Act
named_docs.context: This Act may be cited as the 'Protecting the Information of our Vulnerable Adolescents, Children, and Youth Act'

named_docs.name: Kids PRIVACY Act
named_docs.context: or the 'Kids PRIVACY Act'

named_docs.name: Youth Privacy and Marketing Division Report
named_docs.context: Director of the Youth Privacy and Marketing Division shall submit...a report that includes a description of the work

named_docs.name: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework
named_docs.owner_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
named_docs.context: compliance of the system with the Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework released by the National Institute of Standards and Technology on January 26, 2023, or successor framework

named_docs.name: Study on Accountability Measures for Artificial Intelligence Systems
named_docs.context: The Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information shall conduct a study on accountability measures

named_docs.name: Report on Results of Study on Accountability Measures
named_docs.context: Not later than 18 months after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Assistant Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

named_docs.name: Report on Results of Meetings on Availability of Information
named_docs.context: Not later than 18 months after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Assistant Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

named_docs.name: Report to Congress
named_docs.context: Sec. 19. Report to Congress.

named_docs.name: Pilot Research Program For Sensitive Data
named_docs.context: The Commission shall by rule establish a pilot program that allows vetted, nonprofit, financially disinterested academic institutions and experts to access data

named_docs.name: AI Foundation Model Transparency Act of 2023
named_docs.context: To direct the Federal Trade Commission to establish standards for making publicly available information about the training data and algorithms used in artificial intelligence foundation models

named_docs.context: court cases such as the following: (A) Doe 1 v. GitHub, Inc., No. 22–cv–06823

named_docs.context: This section may be cited as the 'Preventing Adversaries from Developing Critical Capabilities Act'

named_docs.context: Secretary of State, in coordination with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, should develop a strategy to work with the governments of countries that are allies and partners of the United States

named_docs.name: Annual Report on Multilateral Engagement Strategy
named_docs.context: Secretary of State shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, and annually thereafter for 4 years

named_docs.context: Lists foreign persons subject to annual determination and reporting

named_docs.context: This section may be cited as the 'Deterring America's Technological Adversaries Act' or the 'DATA Act'

named_docs.name: Executive Order 14034
named_docs.context: relating to protecting Americans' sensitive data from foreign adversaries

named_docs.context: This Act may be cited as the 'Small Business Artificial Intelligence Training Act of 2024'

named_docs.context: head of each agency shall establish an artificial intelligence strategy for the trustworthy adoption

disambiguation_rules.under secretary: Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Technology (explicitly stated in SEC. 102)
disambiguation_rules.director: Director of the Office of Management and Budget (defined in section (g)(2) as primary meaning unless otherwise indicated)
disambiguation_rules.commission: Resolved to Federal Trade Commission (FTC) via SEC. 2(1) explicit definition
disambiguation_rules.secretary: Resolved to Secretary of Labor per section 13 of this document
disambiguation_rules.assistant secretary: Resolved to Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information as explicitly stated in SEC. 2(a) and SEC. 3(a)
disambiguation_rules.the commission: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) — explicitly referenced throughout SEC. 3 as issuing regulations and guidance
disambiguation_rules.president: Defined in context of this Act as delegating authorities to Secretary of State for multilateral engagement on technology controls
disambiguation_rules.attorney general: Directed by President to seek appropriate relief in district courts for enforcement of this Act
disambiguation_rules.appropriate congressional committees: Defined in section (k)(1) as eight specific committees—five from House, five from Senate
disambiguation_rules.appropriate_congressional_committees: Resolved to eight specific committees: Committee on Foreign Affairs (House), Committee on Financial Services (House), Committee on Foreign Relations (Senate), Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs (Senate) per section (k)(1)
disambiguation_rules.director_of_national_intelligence: Avril Haines identified in SEC. 615(b)(2) as holding this role on December 3, 2022
disambiguation_rules.appropriate_congressional_committees_sec: Defined in section (e)(2) as Committee on Foreign Affairs, Committee on Ways and Means, Committee on Financial Services (House) and Committee on Foreign Relations, Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs (Senate)
disambiguation_rules.appropriate_committees_of_congress: Resolved to four specific committees per SEC. 2(a)(1): Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation (Senate), Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship (Senate), Committee on Science, Space, and Technology (House), Committee on Small Business (House)
disambiguation_rules.relevant_congressional_committees: Defined in SEC. 2(6) as Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs (Senate) and Committee on Oversight and Accountability (House of Representatives)
disambiguation_rules.director_in_sec_4b2: Director of the Office of Management and Budget — per SEC. 2(5) definition; explicitly stated in SEC. 4(b)(2) 'The Director shall issue guidance on the appropriate reporting structure'
disambiguation_rules.relevant_congressional_committees_sec_4c: Resolved to Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs (Senate) and Committee on Oversight and Accountability (House of Representatives) per SEC. 2(6) definition; referenced in SEC. 4(c)(1)

parsing_rules: When a role title includes 'of' + organization name, extract as separate role entity with org field; when legislation creates new regulatory requirements (subchapter IV), extract as enacts ref_type
Subsection (k) Definitions establish formal term definitions; extract all geographic entities listed under 'country of concern' as distinct organizations

oddities.note: Document is a bill preamble/enacting clause only; substantive content sections containing entities referenced in the summary are not provided in this excerpt.

Document excerpt contains only preamble, enacting clause, and bill header. Substantive sections establishing governance requirements, AI charters, oversight provisions, and transparency mechanisms referenced in the summary are not present in this excerpt.
Document contains only enacting clause (Section 1 short title). Substantive provisions regarding AI use in elections, disclaimers, and enforcement mechanisms are not present in this excerpt.
This excerpt contains only the bill introduction and enactment clause; substantive regulatory provisions are not included in the provided text.

Document section contains only short title and table of contents placeholder; no substantive legislative content with extractable entities. The summary indicates key definitions and mandates exist in other sections not provided in this text.

Document contains only introductory and structural content (bill number, introduction date, sponsor information, short title, and table of contents reference). No substantive legislative text extracted in this section. The Federal Digital Platform Commission mentioned in the summary is not explicitly named in this introductory excerpt.

SEC. 615 is a substantive section embedded within Title VI of the Countering Communist China Act. It functions as a standalone legislative enactment (the DATA Act) with its own short title, findings, definitions, and severability clause, despite being a section of a larger Act. The section contains extensive quotations from named intelligence officials but provides no operative legislative language beyond authorization/appropriation and severability provisions.

Document is legislative bill header and enacting clause only; substantive sections not provided in excerpt. Committee assignment indicates jurisdiction over AI governance within government operations and federal agency management.

Document contains only introductory legislative header and enacting clause. Substantive text establishing property rights, prohibitions, damages provisions, First Amendment defense, and limitations period not present in provided excerpt. Full bill text required for comprehensive entity extraction.
